LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONERS THE BUILDER INC., CHARLES BEARD AND RONALD STICKNEY'S MOTION FOR ORDER STAYING PROCEEDINGS - THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION BECAUSE THE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE ARBITRATION AND A STAY. THE COURT DENIES THE MOVING PARTY'S REQUEST FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT IT MADE A REQUEST FOR ARBITRATION AND THAT PLAINTIFF REJECTED THAT REQUEST. A DEMAND FOR ARBITRATION IS A NECESSARY PREREQUISITE UNDER CCP 1281.2. FEES ARE NOT RECOVERABLE BECAUSE THIS MOTION MAY HAVE BEEN AVERTED IF MOVING PARTY MADE THE REQUIRED ARBITRATION DEMAND. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 (302/MJM)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-12-2018 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance, the Court ordered the case dismissed pursuant to Government Code 68608(b). The court finds that a less severe sanction would not be effective in light of the history of failure to comply with the court's orders and rules. Judge: Teri L. Jackson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

Notice Of Motion For Order Staying Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-31-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PETITIONERS THE BUILDER INC., CHARLES BEARD and RONALD STICKNEY's Motion For Order Staying Proceedings - The Court grants the motion because the plaintiff does not oppose arbitration and a stay. The Court denies the moving party's request for attorney's fees. The moving party failed to demonstrate that it made a request for arbitration and that plaintiff rejected that request. A demand for arbitration is a necessary prerequisite under CCP 1281.2. Fees are not recoverable because this motion may have been averted if moving party made the required arbitration demand. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Petition For Compelling Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-31-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS THE BUILDER INC., CHARLES BEARD and RONALD STICKNEY's Petition For Order Compelling Arbitration - The Court grants the motion because the plaintiff does not oppose arbitration and a stay. The Court denies the moving party's request for attorney's fees. The moving party failed to demonstrate that it made a request for arbitration and that plaintiff rejected that request. A demand for arbitration is a necessary prerequisite under CCP 1281.2. Fees are not recoverable because this motion may have been averted if moving party made the required arbitration demand. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
